closureTypes.meaning: Report-only or visibility infrastructure is implemented and validated.
Runtime behavior is confirmed by V2-C evidence gates and explained by V2-D attribution where relevant.
closureTypes.allowedClaim: Narrow runtime claim within the measured protocol.

closureTypes.meaning: Infrastructure is implemented and proven against replay or historical evidence.
Artifact mutation or materialization passed artifact gates but runtime behavior was not confirmed.
A single-screen runtime gate ran and blocked confirmation.
closureTypes.allowedClaim: Negative or blocked screen result only.

closureTypes.meaning: Work cannot proceed because required artifacts, runtime stack, credentials, or policy decisions are unavailable.
Planned work exists but is not implemented.

currentMilestones.claimBoundary: Generated/synthetic/proxy/AOT visibility and safety reports; no generated-class mutation.
Bytecode/classfile footprint reporting; no broad bytecode surgery.
Evidence truth engine replay-proven; not an optimizer.
Memory attribution replay-proven; not an optimizer.
PetClinic runtime-confirmed only for the earlier V2-E reducer protocol.
Productized hardened artifact reducer; no runtime win.
Doctor corrected D2 artifact generalization; runtime not claimed in V2-G.
Hardened PetClinic screen failed promotion; no confirmation.
PetClinic runtime-confirmed for explicit jmoa.reducer.engine=raw protocol.
Raw engine productized and Doctor artifact-smoked; no new runtime claim.
Private Doctor corrected D2 vs raw-reduced D2R runtime-confirmed under Spring Boot fat-JAR/CDS; no public or universal claim.
Public Spring PetClinic visits-service baseline vs raw-reduced baseline runtime-confirmed under exploded-Boot/no-CDS; not a full-P2, startup, fat-JAR/CDS, or universal-service claim.
Report-only reducer admission and recommendation engine proven by 5/5 historical replay; no mutation behavior or new runtime performance claim.
Report-only runtime-policy recommendation engine proven by 7/7 historical replay; no deployment changes, CDS archive training, archive reuse, or new runtime performance claim.

rule: Do not upgrade a closure type by wording alone. Runtime performance claims require fresh V2-C validation and V2-D attribution.
